task_completion.description: A 'Simulated' device adapter of type 'Capnograph' or 'Capnometer' is created in the OpenICE Supervisor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenICE Supervisor window is visible, showing a running device adapter for a Capnograph or Capnometer.

task_completion.description: The 'Respiratory Rate Calculator' application is launched.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Respiratory Rate Calculator' application window is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: A validation report is created at '/home/ga/Desktop/rr_validation_report.txt'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing a file named 'rr_validation_report.txt', or the file icon is visible on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: The validation report includes the device type used.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in the report explicitly mentions 'Capnograph' or 'Capnometer' as the device type.

task_completion.description: The validation report includes the Source RR value and the Calculated RR value.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in the report contains specific numerical values for both the Source RR and the Calculated RR.

task_completion.description: The validation report includes a statement confirming if the values match.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in the report contains a clear statement indicating whether the Source and Calculated RR values match or not.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenICE GUI to observe the values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenICE Supervisor and Respiratory Rate Calculator windows open with actual data, proving the agent didn't just fabricate the report.

integrity.description: The values in the report correspond to the actual values seen in the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Source and Calculated RR values written in the report match the numbers displayed in the OpenICE application windows in the screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Multiparameter Monitor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of a 'Multiparameter Monitor' device adapter being created or used in the OpenICE Supervisor.
